NKJV.1: A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches, Loving favor rather than silver and gold.
NKJV.2: The rich and the poor have this in common, The LORD is the maker of them all.
NKJV.3: A prudent man foresees evil and hides himself, But the simple pass on and are punished.
NKJV.4: By humility and the fear of the LORD Are riches and honor and life.
NKJV.5: Thorns and snares are in the way of the perverse; He who guards his soul will be far from them.
NKJV.6: Train up a child in the way he should go, And when he is old he will not depart from it.
NKJV.7: The rich rules over the poor, And the borrower is servant to the lender.
NKJV.8: He who sows iniquity will reap sorrow, And the rod of his anger will fail.
NKJV.9: He who has a generous eye will be blessed, For he gives of his bread to the poor.
NKJV.10: Cast out the scoffer, and contention will leave; Yes, strife and reproach will cease.
NKJV.11: He who loves purity of heart And has grace on his lips, The king will be his friend.
NKJV.12: The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, But He overthrows the words of the faithless.
NKJV.13: The lazy man says, “There is a lion outside! I shall be slain in the streets!”
NKJV.14: The mouth of an immoral woman is a deep pit; He who is abhorred by the LORD will fall there.
NKJV.15: Foolishness is bound up in the heart of a child; The rod of correction will drive it far from him.
NKJV.16: He who oppresses the poor to increase his riches, And he who gives to the rich, will surely come to poverty.
NKJV.17: Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise, And apply your heart to my knowledge;
NKJV.18: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you; Let them all be fixed upon your lips,
NKJV.19: So that your trust may be in the LORD; I have instructed you today, even you.
NKJV.20: Have I not written to you excellent things Of counsels and knowledge,
NKJV.21: That I may make you know the certainty of the words of truth,  That you may answer words of truth To those who send to you?
NKJV.22: Do not rob the poor because he is poor, Nor oppress the afflicted at the gate;
NKJV.23: For the LORD will plead their cause, And plunder the soul of those who plunder them.
NKJV.24: Make no friendship with an angry man, And with a furious man do not go,
NKJV.25: Lest you learn his ways And set a snare for your soul.
NKJV.26: Do not be one of those who shakes hands in a pledge, One of those who is surety for debts;
NKJV.27: If you have nothing with which to pay, Why should he take away your bed from under you?
NKJV.28: Do not remove the ancient landmark Which your fathers have set.
NKJV.29: Do you see a man who excels in his work? He will stand before kings; He will not stand before unknown men.

ESV.1: A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches, and favor is better than silver or gold.
ESV.2: The rich and the poor meet together; the LORD is the Maker of them all.
ESV.3: The prudent sees danger and hides himself, but the simple go on and suffer for it.
ESV.4: The reward for humility and fear of the LORD is riches and honor and life.
ESV.5: Thorns and snares are in the way of the crooked; whoever guards his soul will keep far from them.
ESV.6: Train up a child in the way he should go; even when he is old he will not depart from it.
ESV.7: The rich rules over the poor, and the borrower is the slave of the lender.
ESV.8: Whoever sows injustice will reap calamity, and the rod of his fury will fail.
ESV.9: Whoever has a bountiful  eye will be blessed, for he shares his bread with the poor.
ESV.10: Drive out a scoffer, and strife will go out, and quarreling and abuse will cease.
ESV.11: He who loves purity of heart, and whose speech is gracious, will have the king as his friend.
ESV.12: The eyes of the LORD keep watch over knowledge, but he overthrows the words of the traitor.
ESV.13: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside! I shall be killed in the streets!”
ESV.14: The mouth of forbidden  women is a deep pit; he with whom the LORD is angry will fall into it.
ESV.15: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child, but the rod of discipline drives it far from him.
ESV.16: Whoever oppresses the poor to increase his own wealth, or gives to the rich, will only come to poverty.
ESV.17: Incline your ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply your heart to my knowledge,
ESV.18: for it will be pleasant if you keep them within you, if all of them are ready on your lips.
ESV.19: That your trust may be in the LORD, I have made them known to you today, even to you.
ESV.20: Have I not written for you thirty sayings of counsel and knowledge,
ESV.21: to make you know what is right and true, that you may give a true answer to those who sent you?
ESV.22: Do not rob the poor, because he is poor, or crush the afflicted at the gate,
ESV.23: for the LORD will plead their cause and rob of life those who rob them.
ESV.24: Make no friendship with a man given to anger, nor go with a wrathful man,
ESV.25: lest you learn his ways and entangle yourself in a snare.
ESV.26: Be not one of those who give pledges, who put up security for debts.
ESV.27: If you have nothing with which to pay, why should your bed be taken from under you?
ESV.28: Do not move the ancient landmark that your fathers have set.
ESV.29: Do you see a man skillful in his work? He will stand before kings; he will not stand before obscure men.

NASB.1: A [good] name is to be more desired than great wealth, Favor is better than silver and gold.
NASB.2: The rich and the poor have a common bond, The LORD is the maker of them all.
NASB.3: The prudent sees the evil and hides himself, But the naive go on, and are punished for it.
NASB.4: The reward of humility [and] the fear of the LORD Are riches, honor and life.
NASB.5: Thorns [and] snares are in the way of the perverse; He who guards himself will be far from them.
NASB.6: Train up a child in the way he should go, Even when he is old he will not depart from it.
NASB.7: The rich rules over the poor, And the borrower [becomes] the lender's slave.
NASB.8: He who sows iniquity will reap vanity, And the rod of his fury will perish.
NASB.9: He who is generous will be blessed, For he gives some of his food to the poor.
NASB.10: Drive out the scoffer, and contention will go out, Even strife and dishonor will cease.
NASB.11: He who loves purity of heart [And] whose speech is gracious, the king is his friend.
NASB.12: The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, But He overthrows the words of the treacherous man.
NASB.13: The sluggard says, 'There is a lion outside; I will be killed in the streets!'
NASB.14: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit; He who is cursed of the LORD will fall into it.
NASB.15: Foolishness is bound up in the heart of a child; The rod of discipline will remove it far from him.
NASB.16: He who oppresses the poor to make more for himself Or who gives to the rich, [will] only [come to] poverty.
NASB.17: Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise, And apply your mind to my knowledge;
NASB.18: For it will be pleasant if you keep them within you, That they may be ready on your lips.
NASB.19: So that your trust may be in the LORD, I have taught you today, even you.
NASB.20: Have I not written to you excellent things Of counsels and knowledge,
NASB.21: To make you know the certainty of the words of truth That you may correctly answer him who sent you?
NASB.22: Do not rob the poor because he is poor, Or crush the afflicted at the gate;
NASB.23: For the LORD will plead their case And take the life of those who rob them.
NASB.24: Do not associate with a man [given] to anger; Or go with a hot-tempered man,
NASB.25: Or you will learn his ways And find a snare for yourself.
NASB.26: Do not be among those who give pledges, Among those who become guarantors for debts.
NASB.27: If you have nothing with which to pay, Why should he take your bed from under you?
NASB.28: Do not move the ancient boundary Which your fathers have set.
NASB.29: Do you see a man skilled in his work? He will stand before kings; He will not stand before obscure men.

NLT.1:
Choose a good reputation over great riches;
being held in high esteem is better than silver or gold.
NLT.2:
The rich and poor have this in common:
The LORD made them both.
NLT.3:
A prudent person foresees danger and takes precautions.
The simpleton goes blindly on and suffers the consequences.
NLT.4:
True humility and fear of the LORD
lead to riches, honor, and long life.
NLT.5:
Corrupt people walk a thorny, treacherous road;
whoever values life will avoid it.
NLT.6:
Direct your children onto the right path,
and when they are older, they will not leave it.
NLT.7:
Just as the rich rule the poor,
so the borrower is servant to the lender.
NLT.8:
Those who plant injustice will harvest disaster,
and their reign of terror will come to an end.
NLT.9:
Blessed are those who are generous,
because they feed the poor.
NLT.10:
Throw out the mocker, and fighting goes, too.
Quarrels and insults will disappear.
NLT.11:
Whoever loves a pure heart and gracious speech
will have the king as a friend.
NLT.12:
The LORD preserves those with knowledge,
but he ruins the plans of the treacherous.
NLT.13:
The lazy person claims, “There’s a lion out there!
If I go outside, I might be killed!”
NLT.14:
The mouth of an immoral woman is a dangerous trap;
those who make the LORD angry will fall into it.
NLT.15:
A youngster’s heart is filled with foolishness,
but physical discipline will drive it far away.
NLT.16:
A person who gets ahead by oppressing the poor
or by showering gifts on the rich will end in poverty.
NLT.17:
Listen to the words of the wise;
apply your heart to my instruction.
NLT.18:
For it is good to keep these sayings in your heart
and always ready on your lips.
NLT.19:
I am teaching you today — yes, you —
so you will trust in the LORD.
NLT.20:
I have written thirty sayings for you,
filled with advice and knowledge.
NLT.21:
In this way, you may know the truth
and take an accurate report to those who sent you.
NLT.22:
Don’t rob the poor just because you can,
or exploit the needy in court.
NLT.23:
For the LORD is their defender.
He will ruin anyone who ruins them.
NLT.24:
Don’t befriend angry people
or associate with hot-tempered people,
NLT.25:
or you will learn to be like them
and endanger your soul.
NLT.26:
Don’t agree to guarantee another person’s debt
or put up security for someone else.
NLT.27:
If you can’t pay it,
even your bed will be snatched from under you.
NLT.28:
Don’t cheat your neighbor by moving the ancient boundary markers
set up by previous generations.
NLT.29:
Do you see any truly competent workers?
They will serve kings
rather than working for ordinary people.

WEB.1: A good name is more desirable than great riches, and loving favor is better than silver and gold.
WEB.2: The rich and the poor have this in common: Yahweh is the maker of them all.
WEB.3: A prudent man sees danger, and hides himself; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.
WEB.4: The result of humility and the fear of Yahweh is wealth, honor, and life.
WEB.5: Thorns and snares are in the path of the wicked: whoever guards his soul stays from them.
WEB.6: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.
WEB.7: The rich rule over the poor. The borrower is servant to the lender.
WEB.8: He who sows wickedness reaps trouble, and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.
WEB.9: He who has a generous eye will be blessed; for he shares his food with the poor.
WEB.10: Drive out the mocker, and strife will go out; yes, quarrels and insults will stop.
WEB.11: He who loves purity of heart and speaks gracefully is the king’s friend.
WEB.12: Yahweh’s eyes watch over knowledge; but he frustrates the words of the unfaithful.
WEB.13: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside! I will be killed in the streets!”
WEB.14: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit. He who is under Yahweh’s wrath will fall into it.
WEB.15: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child: the rod of discipline drives it far from him.
WEB.16: Whoever oppresses the poor for his own increase and whoever gives to the rich, both come to poverty.
WEB.17: Turn your ear, and listen to the words of the wise. Apply your heart to my teaching.
WEB.18: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you, if all of them are ready on your lips.
WEB.19: That your trust may be in Yahweh, I teach you today, even you.
WEB.20: Haven’t I written to you thirty excellent things of counsel and knowledge,
WEB.21: To teach you truth, reliable words, to give sound answers to the ones who sent you?
WEB.22: Don’t exploit the poor, because he is poor; and don’t crush the needy in court;
WEB.23: for Yahweh will plead their case, and plunder the life of those who plunder them.
WEB.24: Don’t befriend a hot-tempered man, and don’t associate with one who harbors anger:
WEB.25: lest you learn his ways, and ensnare your soul.
WEB.26: Don’t you be one of those who strike hands, of those who are collateral for debts.
WEB.27: If you don’t have means to pay, why should he take away your bed from under you?
WEB.28: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone, which your fathers have set up.
WEB.29: Do you see a man skilled in his work? He will serve kings. He won’t serve obscure men.

CSB17.1:
A good name is to be chosen over great wealth;
favor is better than silver and gold. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
Rich and poor have this in common: [1]
the LORD makes them all. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
A sensible person sees danger and takes cover,
but the inexperienced keep going and are punished. ⓝ
CSB17.4:
Humility, the fear of the LORD,
results in wealth, honor, and life. ⓐ
CSB17.5:
There are thorns and snares on the way of the crooked; ⓑ
the one who guards himself stays far from them.
CSB17.6:
Start a youth out on his way; ⓒ
even when he grows old he will not depart from it.
CSB17.7:
The rich rule over the poor, ⓓ
and the borrower is a slave to the lender. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
The one who sows injustice will reap disaster, ⓕ
and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.
CSB17.9:
A generous person [2] will be blessed, ⓖ
for he shares his food with the poor. ⓗ
CSB17.10:
Drive out a mocker, ⓘ and conflict goes too;
then quarreling and dishonor will cease. ⓙ
CSB17.11:
The one who loves a pure heart
and gracious lips — the king is his friend. ⓚ
CSB17.12:
The LORD’s eyes keep watch over knowledge,
but he overthrows the words of the treacherous.
CSB17.13:
The slacker says, “There’s a lion outside!
I’ll be killed in the public square!” ⓛ
CSB17.14:
The mouth of the forbidden woman is a deep pit;
a man cursed by the LORD will fall into it. ⓜ
CSB17.15:
Foolishness is bound to the heart of a youth;
a rod of discipline will separate it from him. ⓝ
CSB17.16:
Oppressing the poor to enrich oneself,
and giving to the rich — both lead only to poverty. ⓐ
CSB17.17:
Listen closely, [3] pay attention to the words of the wise, ⓑ
and apply your mind to my knowledge.
CSB17.18:
For it is pleasing if you keep them within you
and if they are [4] constantly on your lips.
CSB17.19:
I have instructed you today — even you —
so that your confidence may be in the LORD.
CSB17.20:
Haven’t I written for you thirty sayings [5]
about counsel and knowledge,
CSB17.21:
in order to teach you true and reliable words,
so that you may give a dependable report [6] ⓒ
to those who sent you? ⓓ
CSB17.22:
Don’t rob a poor person because he is poor,
and don’t crush the oppressed ⓔ at the city gate, ⓕ
CSB17.23:
for the LORD will champion their cause ⓖ
and will plunder those who plunder them.
CSB17.24:
Don’t make friends with an angry person, [7]
and don’t be a companion of a hot-tempered one, ⓗ
CSB17.25:
or you will learn his ways ⓘ
and entangle yourself in a snare.
CSB17.26:
Don’t be one of those who enter agreements, [8]
who put up security for loans. ⓙ
CSB17.27:
If you have nothing with which to pay,
even your bed will be taken from under you. ⓚ
CSB17.28:
Don’t move an ancient boundary marker
that your fathers set up. ⓛ
CSB17.29:
Do you see a person skilled in his work?
He will stand in the presence of kings.
He will not stand in the presence of the unknown. ⓜ

AMP.1:
A good name [earned by honorable behavior, godly wisdom, moral courage, and personal integrity] is more desirable than great riches;
And favor is better than silver and gold.
AMP.2:
The rich and poor have a common bond;
The LORD is the Maker of them all.
AMP.3:
A prudent and far-sighted person sees the evil [of sin] and hides himself [from it],
But the naive continue on and are punished [by suffering the consequences of sin].
AMP.4:
The reward of humility [that is, having a realistic view of one's importance] and the [reverent, worshipful] fear of the LORD
Is riches, honor, and life.
AMP.5:
Thorns and snares are in the way of the obstinate [for their lack of honor and their wrong-doing traps them];
He who guards himself [with godly wisdom] will be far from them and avoid the consequences they suffer.
AMP.6:
Train up a child in the way he should go [teaching him to seek God's wisdom and will for his abilities and talents],
Even when he is old he will not depart from it.
AMP.7:
The rich rules over the poor,
And the borrower is servant to the lender.
AMP.8:
He who sows injustice will reap [a harvest of] trouble,
And the rod of his wrath [with which he oppresses others] will fail.
AMP.9:
He who is generous will be blessed,
For he gives some of his food to the poor.
AMP.10:
Drive out the scoffer, and contention will go away;
Even strife and dishonor will cease.
AMP.11:
He who loves purity of heart
And whose speech is gracious will have the king as his friend.
AMP.12:
The eyes of the LORD keep guard over knowledge and the one who has it,
But He overthrows the words of the treacherous.
AMP.13:
The lazy one [manufactures excuses and] says, "There is a lion outside!
I will be killed in the streets [if I go out to work]!"
AMP.14:
The mouth of an immoral woman is a deep pit [deep and inescapable];
He who is cursed by the LORD [because of his adulterous sin] will fall into it.
AMP.15:
Foolishness is bound up in the heart of a child;
The rod of discipline [correction administered with godly wisdom and lovingkindness] will remove it far from him.
AMP.16:
He who oppresses or exploits the poor to get more for himself
Or who gives to the rich [to gain influence and favor], will only come to poverty.
AMP.17:
Listen carefully and hear the words of the wise,
And apply your mind to my knowledge;
AMP.18:
For it will be pleasant if you keep them in mind [incorporating them as guiding principles];
Let them be ready on your lips [to guide and strengthen yourself and others].
AMP.19:
So that your trust and reliance and confidence may be in the LORD,
I have taught these things to you today, even to you.
AMP.20:
Have I not written to you excellent things
In counsels and knowledge,
AMP.21:
To let you know the certainty of the words of truth,
That you may give a correct answer to him who sent you?
AMP.22:
Do not rob the poor because he is poor [and defenseless],
Nor crush the afflicted [by legal proceedings] at the gate [where the city court is held],
AMP.23:
For the LORD will plead their case
And take the life of those who rob them.
AMP.24:
Do not even associate with a man given to angry outbursts;
Or go [along] with a hot-tempered man,
AMP.25:
Or you will learn his [undisciplined] ways
And get yourself trapped [in a situation from which it is hard to escape].
AMP.26:
Do not be among those who give pledges [involving themselves in others' finances],
Or among those who become guarantors for others' debts.
AMP.27:
If you have nothing with which to pay [another's debt when he defaults],
Why should his creditor take your bed from under you?
AMP.28:
Do not move the ancient landmark [at the boundary of the property]
Which your fathers have set.
AMP.29:
Do you see a man skillful and experienced in his work?
He will stand [in honor] before kings;
He will not stand before obscure men.

MSG.1:
A sterling reputation is better than striking it rich;
a gracious spirit is better than money in the bank.
MSG.2:
The rich and the poor shake hands as equals—
God made them both!
MSG.3:
A prudent person sees trouble coming and ducks;
a simpleton walks in blindly and is clobbered.
MSG.4:
The payoff for meekness and Fear-of-God
is plenty and honor and a satisfying life.
MSG.5:
The perverse travel a dangerous road, potholed and mud-slick;
if you know what's good for you, stay clear of it.
MSG.6:
Point your kids in the right direction—
when they're old they won't be lost.
MSG.7:
The poor are always ruled over by the rich,
so don't borrow and put yourself under their power.
MSG.8:
Whoever sows sin reaps weeds,
and bullying anger sputters into nothing.
MSG.9:
Generous hands are blessed hands
because they give bread to the poor.
MSG.10:
Kick out the troublemakers and things will quiet down;
you need a break from bickering and griping!
MSG.11:
God loves the pure-hearted and well-spoken;
good leaders also delight in their friendship.
MSG.12:
God guards knowledge with a passion,
but he'll have nothing to do with deception.
MSG.13:
The loafer says, "There's a lion on the loose!
If I go out I'll be eaten alive!"
MSG.14:
The mouth of a whore is a bottomless pit;
you'll fall in that pit if you're on the outs with God.
MSG.15:
Young people are prone to foolishness and fads;
the cure comes through tough-minded discipline.
MSG.16:
Exploit the poor or glad-hand the rich—whichever,
you'll end up the poorer for it.
MSG.17:
Listen carefully to my wisdom;
take to heart what I can teach you.
You'll treasure its sweetness deep within;
MSG.18: you'll give it bold expression in your speech.
MSG.19:
To make sure your foundation is trust in God,
I'm laying it all out right now just for you.
MSG.20:
I'm giving you thirty sterling principles—
tested guidelines to live by.
MSG.21:
Believe me—these are truths that work,
and will keep you accountable
to those who sent you.
MSG.22:
Don't walk on the poor just because they're poor,
and don't use your position to crush the weak,
MSG.23:
Because God will come to their defense;
the life you took, he'll take from you and give back to them.
MSG.24:
Don't hang out with angry people;
don't keep company with hotheads.
MSG.25:
Bad temper is contagious—
don't get infected.
MSG.26:
Don't gamble on the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow,
hocking your house against a lucky chance.
MSG.27:
The time will come when you have to pay up;
you'll be left with nothing but the shirt on your back.
MSG.28:
Don't stealthily move back the boundary lines
staked out long ago by your ancestors.
MSG.29:
Observe people who are good at their work—
skilled workers are always in demand and admired;
they don't take a back seat to anyone.

NIV.1:
A good name is more desirable than great riches;
to be esteemed is better than silver or gold.
NIV.2:
Rich and poor have this in common:
The Lord is the Maker of them all.
NIV.3:
A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge,
but the simple keep going and suffer for it.
NIV.4:
Humility and the fear of the Lord
bring wealth and honor and life.
NIV.5:
In the paths of the wicked lie thorns and snares,
but he who guards his soul stays far from them.
NIV.6:
Train a child in the way he should go,
and when he is old he will not turn from it.
NIV.7:
The rich rule over the poor,
and the borrower is servant to the lender.
NIV.8:
He who sows wickedness reaps trouble,
and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.
NIV.9:
A generous man will himself be blessed,
for he shares his food with the poor.
NIV.10:
Drive out the mocker, and out goes strife;
quarrels and insults are ended.
NIV.11:
He who loves a pure heart and whose speech is gracious
will have the king for his friend.
NIV.12:
The eyes of the Lord keep watch over knowledge,
but he frustrates the words of the unfaithful.
NIV.13:
The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside!”
or, “I will be murdered in the streets!”
NIV.14:
The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit;
he who is under the Lord’s wrath will fall into it.
NIV.15:
Folly is bound up in the heart of a child,
but the rod of discipline will drive it far from him.
NIV.16:
He who oppresses the poor to increase his wealth
and he who gives gifts to the rich—both come to poverty.
NIV.17:
Sayings of the Wise
Pay attention and listen to the sayings of the wise;
apply your heart to what I teach,
NIV.18:
for it is pleasing when you keep them in your heart
and have all of them ready on your lips.
NIV.19:
So that your trust may be in the Lord,
I teach you today, even you.
NIV.20:
Have I not written thirty sayings for you,
sayings of counsel and knowledge,
NIV.21:
teaching you true and reliable words,
so that you can give sound answers
to him who sent you?
NIV.22:
Do not exploit the poor because they are poor
and do not crush the needy in court,
NIV.23:
for the Lord will take up their case
and will plunder those who plunder them.
NIV.24:
Do not make friends with a hot-tempered man,
do not associate with one easily angered,
NIV.25:
or you may learn his ways
and get yourself ensnared.
NIV.26:
Do not be a man who strikes hands in pledge
or puts up security for debts;
NIV.27:
if you lack the means to pay,
your very bed will be snatched from under you.
NIV.28:
Do not move an ancient boundary stone
set up by your forefathers.
NIV.29:
Do you see a man skilled in his work?
He will serve before kings;
he will not serve before obscure men.

NRSVCE.1:
A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches,
and favor is better than silver or gold.
NRSVCE.2:
The rich and the poor have this in common:
the LORD is the maker of them all.
NRSVCE.3:
The clever see danger and hide;
but the simple go on, and suffer for it.
NRSVCE.4:
The reward for humility and fear of the LORD
is riches and honor and life.
NRSVCE.5:
Thorns and snares are in the way of the perverse;
the cautious will keep far from them.
NRSVCE.6:
Train children in the right way,
and when old, they will not stray.
NRSVCE.7:
The rich rule over the poor,
and the borrower is the slave of the lender.
NRSVCE.8:
Whoever sows injustice will reap calamity,
and the rod of anger will fail.
NRSVCE.9:
Those who are generous are blessed,
for they share their bread with the poor.
NRSVCE.10:
Drive out a scoffer, and strife goes out;
quarreling and abuse will cease.
NRSVCE.11:
Those who love a pure heart and are gracious in speech
will have the king as a friend.
NRSVCE.12:
The eyes of the LORD keep watch over knowledge,
but he overthrows the words of the faithless.
NRSVCE.13:
The lazy person says, “There is a lion outside!
I shall be killed in the streets!”
NRSVCE.14:
The mouth of a loose woman is a deep pit;
he with whom the LORD is angry falls into it.
NRSVCE.15:
Folly is bound up in the heart of a boy,
but the rod of discipline drives it far away.
NRSVCE.16:
Oppressing the poor in order to enrich oneself,
and giving to the rich, will lead only to loss.
NRSVCE.17:
The words of the wise:
Incline your ear and hear my words,
and apply your mind to my teaching;
NRSVCE.18:
for it will be pleasant if you keep them within you,
if all of them are ready on your lips.
NRSVCE.19:
So that your trust may be in the LORD,
I have made them known to you today — yes, to you.
NRSVCE.20:
Have I not written for you thirty sayings
of admonition and knowledge,
NRSVCE.21:
to show you what is right and true,
so that you may give a true answer to those who sent you?
NRSVCE.22:
Do not rob the poor because they are poor,
or crush the afflicted at the gate;
NRSVCE.23:
for the LORD pleads their cause
and despoils of life those who despoil them.
NRSVCE.24:
Make no friends with those given to anger,
and do not associate with hotheads,
NRSVCE.25:
or you may learn their ways
and entangle yourself in a snare.
NRSVCE.26:
Do not be one of those who give pledges,
who become surety for debts.
NRSVCE.27:
If you have nothing with which to pay,
why should your bed be taken from under you?
NRSVCE.28:
Do not remove the ancient landmark
that your ancestors set up.
NRSVCE.29:
Do you see those who are skillful in their work?
They will serve kings;
they will not serve common people.

NET.1: A good name is to be chosen rather than great wealth, good favor more than silver or gold.
NET.2: The rich and the poor meet together; the LORD is the creator of them both.
NET.3: A shrewd person sees danger and hides himself, but the naive keep right on going and suffer for it.
NET.4: The reward for humility and fearing the LORD is riches and honor and life.
NET.5: Thorns and snares are in the path of the perverse, but the one who guards himself keeps far from them.
NET.6: Train a child in the way that he should go, and when he is old he will not turn from it.
NET.7: The rich rule over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.
NET.8: The one who sows iniquity will reap trouble, and the rod of his fury will end.
NET.9: A generous person will be blessed, for he gives some of his food to the poor.
NET.10: Drive out the scorner and contention will leave; strife and insults will cease.
NET.11: The one who loves a pure heart and whose speech is gracious — the king will be his friend.
NET.12: The eyes of the LORD guard knowledge, but he overthrows the words of the faithless person.
NET.13: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside! I will be killed in the middle of the streets!”
NET.14: The mouth of an adulteress is like a deep pit; the one against whom the LORD is angry will fall into it.
NET.15: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child, but the rod of discipline will drive it far from him.
NET.16: The one who oppresses the poor to increase his own gain and the one who gives to the rich — both end up only in poverty.
NET.17: Incline your ear and listen to the words of the wise, and apply your heart to my instruction.
NET.18: For it is pleasing if you keep these sayings within you, and they are ready on your lips.
NET.19: So that your confidence may be in the LORD, I am making them known to you today — even you.
NET.20: Have I not written thirty sayings for you, sayings of counsel and knowledge,
NET.21: to show you true and reliable words, so that you may give accurate answers to those who sent you?
NET.22: Do not exploit a poor person because he is poor and do not crush the needy in court,
NET.23: for the LORD will plead their case and will rob those who are robbing them.
NET.24: Do not make friends with an angry person, and do not associate with a wrathful person,
NET.25: lest you learn his ways and entangle yourself in a snare.
NET.26: Do not be one who strikes hands in pledge or who puts up security for debts.
NET.27: If you do not have enough to pay, your bed will be taken right out from under you!
NET.28: Do not move an ancient boundary stone which was put in place by your ancestors.
NET.29: Do you see a person skilled in his work? He will take his position before kings; he will not take his position before obscure people.
